stories.title: Trump pauses a planned Iran strike and reopens a negotiation window
stories.summary: Donald Trump said he called off a planned Tuesday attack on Iran to give fresh talks a chance after pressure from Gulf allies. The move lowers the immediate risk of a wider war, but markets and regional governments are treating it as a fragile pause rather than a durable de-escalation.

links.headline: Live updates: Trump calls off Iran strike planned for Tuesday
links.excerpt: AP tracks the White House shift from imminent military action to a last-minute diplomatic pause.
links.imageAlt: Live updates: Trump calls off Iran strike planned for Tuesday

links.headline: Trump delays 'scheduled attack' on Iran, crediting 'serious negotiations'
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera adds regional political reaction and the domestic pressure Trump is facing over the Iran war.
links.imageAlt: Trump delays 'scheduled attack' on Iran, crediting 'serious negotiations'

links.headline: Trump says he cancelled planned attack on Iran at request of Gulf states
links.excerpt: The Guardian emphasizes the role Gulf leaders played in pushing for another attempt at talks.
links.imageAlt: Trump says he cancelled planned attack on Iran at request of Gulf states

stories.imageAlt: Live updates: Trump calls off Iran strike planned for Tuesday

stories.title: Trump-Xi summit aftermath produces farm-trade gains but little strategic clarity
stories.summary: Washington and Beijing are presenting new agricultural trade commitments as evidence the summit stabilized the relationship. But the harder questions around Taiwan, Iran and broader strategic rivalry remain unresolved, leaving the headline deal narrower than the optics suggest.

links.headline: China to boost trade for US beef and poultry
links.excerpt: AP focuses on the White House claim that China will sharply increase agricultural purchases after the summit.
links.imageAlt: China to boost trade for US beef and poultry

links.headline: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war
links.excerpt: ABC frames the visit as diplomatically warm but substantively thin on the biggest geopolitical files.
links.imageAlt: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war

links.headline: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI
links.excerpt: The Guardian ties the farm deal to the wider failure to land clear progress on security and technology disputes.
links.imageAlt: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI

stories.imageAlt: China to boost trade for US beef and poultry

stories.title: Belarus and Russia stage nuclear drills that sharpen warnings on NATO's eastern flank
stories.summary: Belarus has begun drills with Russia centered on nuclear weapons use and deployment, drawing alarm from Kyiv and NATO-border states. The exercises reinforce the sense that Moscow is using Belarus both as a military pressure point and as a venue for constant escalation signaling.

links.headline: Belarus launches joint drills with Russia to practice nuclear weapons use
links.excerpt: AP covers the launch of the exercises and the immediate Ukrainian and regional reaction.
links.imageAlt: Belarus launches joint drills with Russia to practice nuclear weapons use

links.headline: Belarus dismisses concerns as it launches joint nuclear drills with Russia
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera summarizes Minsk's message that the training is defensive while highlighting outside condemnation.
links.imageAlt: Belarus dismisses concerns as it launches joint nuclear drills with Russia

links.headline: Belarus launches drills involving Russian nuclear weapons
links.excerpt: DW adds European security context and the concern that Belarus remains a staging area for future pressure on Ukraine or NATO.
links.imageAlt: Belarus launches drills involving Russian nuclear weapons

stories.imageAlt: Belarus launches joint drills with Russia to practice nuclear weapons use

stories.title: Pressure grows on Israel over Gaza as war deaths mount and legal warnings sharpen
stories.summary: Fresh Israeli strikes in Gaza are colliding with intensifying legal and diplomatic pressure over civilian harm and humanitarian access. The immediate battlefield story is still about ongoing strikes and Hamas targeting claims, but the broader frame is shifting toward accountability and genocide-prevention obligations.

links.headline: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says
links.excerpt: AP leads with Israel's claim that a senior Hamas military figure was the intended target.
links.imageAlt: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says

links.headline: Three community kitchen workers among five killed by Israel in Gaza
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera concentrates on the latest strike deaths and the hit to already strained relief work.
links.imageAlt: Three community kitchen workers among five killed by Israel in Gaza

links.headline: UN demands Israel prevent genocide in Gaza
links.excerpt: Ahram Online summarizes the new UN human rights report and the genocide-prevention language now shaping the debate.
links.imageAlt: UN demands Israel prevent genocide in Gaza

stories.imageAlt: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says

stories.title: Greenland tells Trump's envoy that sovereignty is not up for negotiation
stories.summary: Greenland's prime minister used a meeting with Trump's envoy to restate that the island's future belongs to Greenlanders and cannot be bargained away. The exchange matters because Washington's pressure campaign has not disappeared, even if the tone of the talks is now more diplomatic.

links.headline: Greenland's Prime Minister Nielsen asserts self-determination in meeting with Trump envoy
links.excerpt: AP reports the blunt sovereignty message Greenland delivered directly to the US delegation.
links.imageAlt: Greenland's Prime Minister Nielsen asserts self-determination in meeting with Trump envoy

links.headline: Greenland's prime minister says US still wants to acquire territory
links.excerpt: The Guardian focuses on Nielsen's view that Washington's underlying objective has not changed.
links.imageAlt: Greenland's prime minister says US still wants to acquire territory

links.headline: Greenland says US talks make progress but island is not for sale
links.excerpt: This Reuters-carried report adds the line that talks may be calmer, but the red line on sovereignty is unchanged.
links.imageAlt: Greenland says US talks make progress but island is not for sale

stories.imageAlt: Greenland's Prime Minister Nielsen asserts self-determination in meeting with Trump envoy

stories.title: US and Nigeria expand strikes after killing a senior Islamic State leader
stories.summary: The killing of a senior Islamic State figure in Nigeria has been followed by additional coordinated strikes, signaling a more assertive US-Nigeria counterterror campaign. The operation is strategically notable because it ties a high-profile militant decapitation to a broader effort to disrupt Islamic State networks in West Africa.

links.headline: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint US-Nigerian mission
links.excerpt: AP covers the original operation and the US claim that the target was among Islamic State's top global figures.
links.imageAlt: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint US-Nigerian mission

links.headline: US military carries out more strikes against ISIL fighters in Nigeria
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera follows the operation into the next round of strikes and what officials say they were meant to achieve.
links.imageAlt: US military carries out more strikes against ISIL fighters in Nigeria

links.headline: US-Nigeria Coordinated Strike Against ISIS Fighters
links.excerpt: AFRICOM's statement is the primary source on the follow-on strike and the official damage assessment language.

stories.imageAlt: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint US-Nigerian mission

stories.title: Kenya fuel protests turn deadly as oil-shock pain hits consumers directly
stories.summary: Fuel price anger in Kenya has escalated into deadly protests and transport disruption, turning the global oil shock into a domestic political crisis. The unrest is a reminder that the economic fallout from wider conflicts is now landing hardest in countries with thinner fiscal and energy buffers.

links.headline: 4 people killed and dozens injured in Kenya protests over record fuel prices
links.excerpt: AP gives the clearest account of the casualties and the scale of the national transport strike.
links.imageAlt: 4 people killed and dozens injured in Kenya protests over record fuel prices

links.headline: Four reported killed, 30 injured, in Kenyan fuel price protests
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera adds broader political context from Kenya's recent protest cycles and policing tensions.
links.imageAlt: Four reported killed, 30 injured, in Kenyan fuel price protests

links.headline: Two shot dead as fuel protests paralyse transport in western Kenya
links.excerpt: The Standard supplies more local detail on how the demonstrations spread beyond Nairobi.
links.imageAlt: Two shot dead as fuel protests paralyse transport in western Kenya

stories.imageAlt: 4 people killed and dozens injured in Kenya protests over record fuel prices

stories.title: Sinaloa cartel corruption claims put new strain on US-Mexico relations
stories.summary: The surrender of a former Sinaloa security chief has intensified scrutiny of alleged cartel-state ties and raised the stakes for Mexico's federal leadership. What could have been a single court development is now feeding a bigger cross-border argument about organized crime, sovereignty and political accountability.

links.headline: Ex-Sinaloa security chief is first of 10 indicted Mexican officials to surrender to US authorities
links.excerpt: AP lays out the US case and why the arrest matters beyond one former official.
links.imageAlt: Ex-Sinaloa security chief is first of 10 indicted Mexican officials to surrender to US authorities

links.headline: Pressure on Mexico after two ex-officials surrender to US over alleged cartel ties
links.excerpt: The Guardian emphasizes the pressure now falling on President Sheinbaum and Morena.

links.headline: Mexico freezes bank accounts of ex-officials investigated by US
links.excerpt: This Reuters-carried report adds Mexico's immediate response and the financial fallout for the accused officials.
links.imageAlt: Mexico freezes bank accounts of ex-officials investigated by US

links.headline: Ex-Sinaloa security chief is first indicted Mexican official to surrender to U.S.
links.excerpt: The Los Angeles Times adds more detail on the cross-border legal and political implications.
links.imageAlt: Ex-Sinaloa security chief is first indicted Mexican official to surrender to U.S.

stories.imageAlt: Ex-Sinaloa security chief is first of 10 indicted Mexican officials to surrender to US authorities

stories.title: Oil and equity markets remain volatile as traders price diplomacy and war risk at once
stories.summary: Markets are swinging between relief over delayed military escalation and fear that the Iran conflict could still disrupt supply and transport. The pattern now is less about one-off panic and more about sustained uncertainty spilling through oil, bonds and risk assets globally.

links.headline: Oil prices keep swinging, and so do stocks worldwide
links.excerpt: AP gives the broadest read on how crude, equities and rate-sensitive sectors moved through the day.
links.imageAlt: Oil prices keep swinging, and so do stocks worldwide

links.headline: Oil prices keep swinging, and so do stocks worldwide
links.excerpt: The Los Angeles Times mirrors the market story with emphasis on how fast prices reversed after Trump's late statement.
links.imageAlt: Oil prices keep swinging, and so do stocks worldwide

links.headline: Investors bet on stability after Trump-Xi summit as Iran war concerns linger
links.excerpt: This Reuters-carried market piece adds the investor view that summit stability is real but limited.
links.imageAlt: Investors bet on stability after Trump-Xi summit as Iran war concerns linger

stories.imageAlt: Oil prices keep swinging, and so do stocks worldwide

stories.title: OpenAI wins a major courtroom fight as Musk's case is thrown out on timing grounds
stories.summary: A federal jury sided with OpenAI and Sam Altman, concluding Elon Musk waited too long to bring his claims. The verdict removes a major legal threat hanging over OpenAI's structure and future capital plans, while leaving the broader ideological fight over AI governance unresolved.

links.headline: Court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman
links.excerpt: AP captures the central holding: Musk's case failed because the claims were brought too late.
links.imageAlt: Court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman

links.headline: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera summarizes what the loss means for the high-profile Musk-Altman feud over AI's direction.
links.imageAlt: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI

links.headline: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI
links.excerpt: TechCrunch focuses on the downstream business impact for OpenAI and the AI sector more broadly.
links.imageAlt: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI

stories.imageAlt: Court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman

stories.title: US senators summon major platform chiefs for another child-safety showdown
stories.summary: Meta, Alphabet, TikTok and Snap chiefs are being called back to Capitol Hill as lawmakers renew pressure over social media harms to children. The hearing shows the issue has not cooled politically and keeps regulatory heat on the biggest consumer internet platforms.

links.headline: Meta, Alphabet, TikTok and Snap CEOs called to testify in Senate
links.excerpt: AP covers the hearing notice and the latest phase of congressional pressure on platform executives.
links.imageAlt: Meta, Alphabet, TikTok and Snap CEOs called to testify in Senate

links.headline: Scoop: Tech CEOs summoned to Capitol for June hearing
links.excerpt: Axios adds reporting on the timing and why lawmakers see the child-safety fight as intensifying.

links.headline: Social media CEOs called to Senate Judiciary hearing
links.excerpt: Roll Call adds congressional detail on the lines of questioning senators want to pursue.
links.imageAlt: Social media CEOs called to Senate Judiciary hearing

stories.imageAlt: Meta, Alphabet, TikTok and Snap CEOs called to testify in Senate

stories.title: Australia's under-16 social media ban is now colliding with news access
stories.summary: New research suggests Australia's social media ban is also cutting teenagers off from news exposure, not just platform harms. That complicates a policy once framed mostly as child safety, because it now raises a second-order problem around civic information and media reach.

links.headline: Australia wants to sell its social media ban to the world – but are the measures even working?
links.excerpt: The Guardian adds broader scrutiny of whether the ban is achieving its goals even before the new research landed.
links.imageAlt: Australia wants to sell its social media ban to the world – but are the measures even working?

links.headline: Teens consuming less news since social media ban: research
links.excerpt: ABC's current write-up focuses directly on the finding that teens are not replacing social feeds with other news sources.
links.imageAlt: Teens consuming less news since social media ban: research

links.headline: Social media ban means teenagers are getting less news
links.excerpt: Crikey argues the ban may be producing disengagement rather than a shift to healthier news habits.
links.imageAlt: Social media ban means teenagers are getting less news

stories.imageAlt: Teens consuming less news since social media ban: research

stories.title: Northern Territory youth justice changes revive alarm over police powers and child detention
stories.summary: The Northern Territory government wants police to be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question some without an adult present. Supporters frame the bill as a public-safety response, but critics see it as another step toward normalizing harsher treatment of vulnerable children, especially Indigenous youths.

links.headline: Children to be held in police watch houses for up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes
links.excerpt: ABC provides the clearest detail on the bill's changes and the legal concerns raised by advocates.
links.imageAlt: Children to be held in police watch houses for up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes

links.headline: NT government accused of renaming watch house to hide ongoing long-term detentions
links.excerpt: This ABC backgrounder helps explain why rights groups are reacting so sharply to the new detention proposal.
links.imageAlt: NT government accused of renaming watch house to hide ongoing long-term detentions

links.headline: Meeting Documents – Northern Territory Government – Legislative Assembly
links.excerpt: The assembly documents page is the official reference point for current papers and draft Hansard around the bill.

stories.imageAlt: Children to be held in police watch houses for up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes

stories.title: Queensland's restored developer donations are already flowing back into politics
stories.summary: Queensland's reversal of a ban on property developer donations is now producing a visible wave of money into state politics. The story matters because it turns an abstract electoral-law change into a live test of whether old corruption safeguards are being quickly unwound.

links.headline: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash
links.excerpt: ABC quantifies how quickly developers resumed giving after the ban was lifted.
links.imageAlt: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

links.headline: Lifting ban on developer donations could increase corruption risk, Queensland watchdog says
links.excerpt: This earlier ABC piece is useful context on the anti-corruption concerns that critics warned about before the change.
links.imageAlt: Lifting ban on developer donations could increase corruption risk, Queensland watchdog says

links.headline: State government elections fact sheet
links.excerpt: The ECQ fact sheet is the primary reference for the state's donation rules and prohibited-donor framework.

stories.imageAlt: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash

stories.title: Sara Duterte's impeachment trial opens in a far more unstable Senate than expected
stories.summary: The Philippine Senate has formally convened as an impeachment court for Vice President Sara Duterte, but it is doing so amid leadership turmoil, recent gunfire inside the Senate compound and deeper factional conflict. The trial is no longer just a legal proceeding; it has become a test of institutional control in Manila.

links.headline: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages
links.excerpt: AP captures the opening of the trial and the extraordinary instability surrounding it.
links.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages

links.headline: Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial amid political division
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera links the trial to the shootout, leadership shifts and the wider Duterte-Marcos split.
links.imageAlt: Philippines launches VP Duterte impeachment trial amid political division

links.headline: Senators don robes as Duterte impeach court opens
links.excerpt: Philstar adds the domestic political texture and procedural details from inside the chamber.
links.imageAlt: Senators don robes as Duterte impeach court opens

stories.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages

stories.title: Power alerts in the Visayas keep exposing the Philippines' electricity fragility
stories.summary: Fresh yellow alerts in the Visayas are reinforcing how little margin exists in parts of the Philippine grid. The issue is bigger than one day's outage risk because it feeds into political scrutiny of energy reliability, plant maintenance and dry-season demand planning.

links.headline: Visayas grid on yellow alert again; Palace, solons seek probes
links.excerpt: BusinessMirror covers the renewed alert and the immediate political calls for investigation.

links.headline: More grid alerts expected as El Nino progresses
links.excerpt: BusinessWorld gives the forward-looking system view, not just the latest advisory.
links.imageAlt: More grid alerts expected as El Nino progresses

links.headline: NGCP extends Visayas power alert
links.excerpt: Philstar's Cebu reporting adds the regional on-the-ground impact from the extended alert.

stories.imageAlt: More grid alerts expected as El Nino progresses
